desc_first: Extends 20 bAbI reasoning tasks (fact chaining, induction, deduction, counting, lists) by scattering relevant sentences across PG-19 book text.

name: RULER
desc_first: NVIDIA.

name: HELMET
desc_first: Princeton NLP.

name: InfiniteBench (∞Bench)
desc_first: Tsinghua / OpenBMB.

name: LongBench
desc_first: Tsinghua THUDM.

name: MemBench
desc_first: Renmin University.

name: MemoryAgentBench
desc_first: HUST.

name: MemoryArena
desc_first: Stanford Digital Economy Lab et al.

name: MemoryBench (Tsinghua THUIR)
desc_first: Tsinghua THUIR.

name: Zep / Graphiti
desc_first: Memory and observability in one.

name: Galileo (galileo.ai)
desc_first: Treats memory as first-class in multi-agent tracing.

name: Langfuse
memory_model: Memory ops as named spans + Agent Graphs
desc_first: Memory module reads/writes captured as named spans.

name: Mem0 (with AgentOps)
desc_first: When integrated with AgentOps, surfaces Memory Operation Timeline, Search Analytics (query patterns, hit rates), Memory Growth curves, Cost Attribution per memory op.

name: Ratine
desc_first: Only tool found that scans the persistent memory layer on disk rather than runtime tracing.

name: AgentOps
desc_first: When Mem0 is connected, gains Memory Operation Timeline, Search Analytics, Memory Growth tracking, Error Tracking per memory call.

name: LangSmith
memory_model: Memory mutations as distinct span types
desc_first: Memory reads, vector DB retrievals, state changes are distinct span types in traces.

name: Lakera Guard / Lakera Red
desc_first: Screens every prompt, response, and retrieved document for indirect prompt injection — primary vector for memory poisoning.

name: OWASP Agent Memory Guard
desc_first: Open-source runtime defense.

name: Microsoft Agent Governance Toolkit
desc_first: Cross-Model Verification Kernel (CMVK) requires majority-voting agreement across multiple model calls before a memory-influenced action.

name: Mem0 Security / OpenMemory
desc_first: Commercial Mem0 ships SOC 2 / HIPAA, zero-trust access controls, BYOK encryption, real-time monitoring, audit logs, workspace governance as defaults.

name: Enkrypt AI
desc_first: Applies guardrails at three points: (1) before write to vector DB, (2) before query reaches embedding model, (3) before response.

name: Acuvity (now Proofpoint)
desc_first: Runtime enforcement targeting memory poisoning, unauthorised execution, identity spoofing per the OWASP LLM threat list.

name: HiddenLayer AISec Platform 2.0
desc_first: Targets the supply-chain / lineage layer rather than runtime memory writes.

name: Zep — governance posture
desc_first: Bi-temporal knowledge graph preserves full lineage of every stored fact — every memory write traceable and auditable by graph schema, not bolted-on logging.

name: From Human Memory to AI Memory (survey)
section: Theoretical / informal — ideas without a paper
desc_first: Eight-quadrant classification grid across personal/system, parametric/non-parametric, and short-term/long-term axes.

name: Externalization in LLM Agents
section: Theoretical / informal — ideas without a paper
memory_model: Memory + skills + protocols as coupled externalization
desc_first: Traces the shift from weights-as-capability to harness-as-capability; analyzes memory, skills, and protocols as three coupled forms of externalization and examines how they interact.

name: Memory as Metabolism
section: Theoretical / informal — ideas without a paper
memory_model: Metabolic governance cycle for companion knowledge
desc_first: Argues that companion AI knowledge systems must treat memory as a continuous metabolic process rather than a static store.

name: LLM as OS Kernel
section: Theoretical / informal — ideas without a paper
desc_first: LLM is "the kernel process of a new Operating System" orchestrating I/O, code interpreter, browser, and an embedding store for files / internal memory.

name: LLM OS Specs
section: Theoretical / informal — ideas without a paper
desc_first: Operationalises the kernel analogy: 256-core processor at 20 Hz (tokens/sec), RAM = 128K tokens of context, filesystem = Ada-002 embedding store.

name: Context Engineering
section: Theoretical / informal — ideas without a paper
desc_first: "+1 for 'context engineering' over 'prompt engineering' … the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." The LLM is "a coworker with anterograde amnesia" — cannot consolidate or build long-running knowledge once training ends.

name: LLM Wiki — compiled memory
section: Theoretical / informal — ideas without a paper
desc_first: Three-layer directory: raw/ (immutable sources), wiki/ (LLM-maintained compiled pages), CLAUDE.md (schema).

name: Four-Type Agent Memory Taxonomy
section: Theoretical / informal — ideas without a paper
desc_first: "Agent = LLM + memory + planning + tools." Maps cognitive memory types to LLM machinery: sensory (learned embeddings), short-term / in-context (the context window), long-term (external vector store with fast retrieval).

name: Context Expansion Law
section: Theoretical / informal — ideas without a paper
desc_first: "Application context tends to expand to fill the context limits supported by the model." Treats agent memory as a first-class unresolved design problem rather than a solved component; explicitly defers a memory deep-dive to a future post.

name: Files Are All You Need
section: Theoretical / informal — ideas without a paper
desc_first: Coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor) converge on the filesystem as their primary memory abstraction: conversation histories as searchable files, skills as files, retrieval via file search rather than vector DBs.

name: RL-Based Continual Learning as Memory
section: Theoretical / informal — ideas without a paper
desc_first: On-policy RL is naturally more resistant to catastrophic forgetting than supervised fine-tuning — its online nature biases learning toward low-distribution-shift updates.

name: Memory Poisoning as Attack Surface
section: Theoretical / informal — ideas without a paper
desc_first: Agent persistent memory is an attack surface: an adversarial document in the environment can instruct the agent's memory tool to write malicious facts, poisoning the long-term store.

name: Context Engineering — naming event
section: Theoretical / informal — ideas without a paper
desc_first: Endorses "context engineering" as a distinct discipline from prompt engineering — what agents do with their context window (routing, compression, tool output formatting, memory retrieval injection) is engineering, not just prompting.

name: JEPA Six-Module World Model (informal)
section: Theoretical / informal — ideas without a paper
desc_first: Six-module cognitive architecture (perception, world model, cost, memory, action, configurator).
